And you know, I often get a question what's the difference between affiliates and joint venture partners. And in reality, there isn't very much difference. is a lot simpler than a big JV launch. So that's sort of where the internal launch fits. It's bigger results in the seed launch, but simpler than the JV launch. Even though you're reusing your sideways sales letter from your internal launch, when you do that JV launch, it's it is a bunch of work. The internal launch is the real bread and butter in the PLF world. So this is, I love them because they're so much easier than JV launches. And, you know, the really the majority of launches out there that PLF owners are doing tend to be these, these internal launches. I consider them very elegant launches. And the reason they're elegant is you do have maximum control, they happen on your time frame. So, for instance, let's say, you know, you have that classic sideways sales letter, where you have three videos, you know, say you release video one, and you know, day one of your pre-launch, and video two on day four of your pre-launch, and I'm going to get into all this later on in the next module, all the timing and stuff. And then, but let's just say day one, four, and seven are the days you release your videos. So day one, you release video one, day four, you release video two, and day seven, you release video three. Well, in an internal launch, that means on day one, you're mailing your list. And then on day four, about PLC one. And then on day four, you're mailing your list about PLC two. And on day seven, you're mailing your list about PLC three, and you're setting up for that sales video. And you're controlling exactly when those emails go out. And it's real nice that way. Now, if you have a JV launch, you don't know exactly when your partner's going to mail. You know, they might wait until day three to mail video one. And they might

wait until day seven to mail video two. In fact, they might wait till day eight to mail video one. You just never know exactly when your partners are going to mail. But then another big deal is like, once you start to do JV launches, you sort of have to plan your time, your planning horizon extends out quite a bit further, because you have to plan it for time when your partners have space in their publication calendar. And you have to warn them ahead of time. Like if you want to do an internal launch next Monday, and you know, it's Thursday, and you can knock out those the PLC one by Monday, you could do it. You could just you could just spend the weekend knocking out PLC one, and go ahead and send that email on Monday. And then you'd be scrambling to do PLC two and PLC three, but you could do it. But with a JV launch, you can't just like on Thursday mail your partners and say, Hey, I'm doing a JV launch on Monday, can you mail for me? You could do it. But the the odds of them actually being able to have room in their calendar and time to put the promotion together, they're probably not going to. So you have to plan on a much longer time frame. it takes two or three weeks, and I've done it several times. And when I do it, I actually take my own boat, no guides whatsoever. It's just my boat. Now, just a couple questions, I know they typically pop up at this point, people that wonder, how big a list do you need for an internal launch? And I would think at least, you know, several hundred people, you know, three, four hundred people, you know, it depends like so many things. The answer is really it depends. If, for instance, you're selling, you know, a real high end product with a high end price of $1,000 or $2,000 or even more, then, you know, a smaller list, if you have a, you know, 300 people and they're engaged, you're probably going to be able to generate enough sales where it's going to do pretty well. But if you're selling like a product for, you know, $19 or $47, maybe a list of 300 people, then you just might your conversions, you're just not going to convert enough people to make that launch really, really attractive. Now, the good news is if you're in a low, you know, type of niche or market where there's lower prices, then it's usually easier to build a bigger list because it's a bigger market. That's the way it is. The smaller markets generally have small, higher prices and bigger markets tend to have higher prices or lower prices. So for example, if you're doing a product in time management, it's just straight up time management. Well, there's a lot of people that are interested in that. It's going to be easier to build a bigger list, but it's probably going to get a lower price point than if you had a product that was like time management for chiropractic, no time management for plastic surgeons, you know, because then you're going to have a smaller market, but it's probably going to be a higher price product, even if the stuff in the product is fairly similar. So it's taking you six weeks to deliver it. You know, then maybe it's going to take you a few weeks to get the transcripts done and put it together into a real product.
